title: U.S. Flies 17 Migrants to CECOT Despite Judge Murphy's TRO — Government Whistleblower Alleges Deliberate Violation
detail: On March 31, the United States flew 17 individuals it alleged — without presenting individual evidence — were members of Tren de Aragua and MS-13 on U.S. military aircraft to El Salvador's CECOT mega-prison. The deportation occurred despite U.S. District Judge Brian E. Murphy having issued a temporary restraining order on March 28 barring deportations to third countries without a meaningful opportunity to raise Convention Against Torture claims. Legal analysts concluded the government transferred ICE detainees to Department of Defense custody before the flights, apparently to argue the TRO's named defendant (ICE) was technically not involved. A government whistleblower, DOJ attorney Erez Reuveni, subsequently alleged the deportations were carried out in knowing violation of the court's order and was suspended by DOJ following his disclosure. The incident added to growing federal court concern about executive defiance of judicial orders in immigration cases.

media.caption: Immigration enforcement continues amid nationwide protests and court challenges

title: LAist Analysis: ICE Arrests Tripled in Los Angeles in 2025 — 14,000 Arrests, 39% With No Criminal Record
detail: LAist published an analysis on March 31 of ICE enforcement data showing over 14,000 arrests in the greater Los Angeles area in 2025 — three times the 4,681 arrests in 2024. Of those arrested, 39% had no criminal charges or convictions at time of arrest, consistent with national TRAC data showing 73.6% of ICE detainees holding no criminal conviction. The analysis drew on ICE's publicly released arrest records and highlighted the geographic concentration of enforcement in majority-Latino communities in East LA, the San Fernando Valley, and the Inland Empire. The data was cited by advocates and state officials calling for expanded sanctuary protections and state-level oversight of ICE operations.

title: DHS Announces Arrests of Violent Criminal Aliens Across New Jersey, Utah, California, and Texas
detail: DHS issued a 'Make America Safe Again' press release on March 31 announcing the arrest of multiple undocumented individuals with violent criminal records. Named cases included Carlos Capurro (Peruvian national, Essex County, NJ — convicted of aggravated sexual assault of a child under 13), Martin Feliciano Barragan-Rojas (Mexican national, Hurricane, UT — convicted of aggravated kidnapping and assault), Sergio Javier Portillo-Perez (Salvadoran national, Fairfield, CA — assault with a deadly weapon), Francisco Velardo-Vasquez (Mexican national, Van Nuys, CA — assault with a firearm), and Shawn Kaleffe Graham (Jamaican national, Galveston County, TX — robbery). The press release was part of a consistent DHS communications strategy of publicizing criminal enforcement actions as Congress debated ICE accountability legislation tied to the DHS partial shutdown.

title: No Kings Protest in Honolulu: 15-Year-Old Arrested After Attack on Man Wearing ICE Gear
detail: On March 31, during a 'No Kings' demonstration in Honolulu, Hawaii, footage emerged showing a man wearing ICE-style gear being attacked by protesters. A 15-year-old was arrested on a second-degree assault charge in connection with the incident. The Honolulu protest was part of ongoing nationwide post-March 28 demonstrations that had drawn an estimated 8–9 million participants across more than 3,300 events on March 28 alone — described by organizers as the largest single-day protest in U.S. history. Interfaith Alliance and partner organizations hosted a national interfaith town hall on March 31 to reflect on the protests and plan next steps, including a May 1 national Mayday demonstration.
